cxu_object.claim: The training instruct endpoint interprets sell as open_short not reduce_long so the spike catcher must use action close to reduce a long position otherwise it flips the position to short and loses money on the continuing spike

supporting_contexts.text: On 2026-04-17 the spike catcher detected a spike up and sent action=sell sizePct=50 via training/instruct. This opened a SHORT position instead of reducing the existing LONG. The spike continued up and the new short lost $145+. The fix: use action=close for partial position reduction, not action=sell.
